item.title: Fiji Showcase 2026 attracts record turnout of 100,000 visitors
item.description: More than 100,000 people attended Fiji Showcase 2026 over its nine-day run, making it the biggest event in the show's history and setting a new attendance record for Communications Fiji Limited (CFL). CFL General Manager Charles Taylor says the milestone is the highest attendance ever achieved since the event began as the Home and Leisure Show at the Suva Civic Centre in 1986.
item.content:encoded:
More than 100,000 people attended Fiji Showcase 2026 over its nine-day run, making it the biggest event in the show's history and setting a new attendance record for Communications Fiji Limited (CFL).

CFL General Manager Charles Taylor says the milestone is the highest attendance ever achieved since the event began as the Home and Leisure Show at the Suva Civic Centre in 1986.

Taylor says the record reflects the hard work and commitment of the entire CFL team, who spent months planning and nine demanding days delivering the event.

He also highlighted that this year's Fiji Showcase was blessed with nine consecutive days of fine weather, the first time that has happened in the event's history.

Taylor says while the event was exhausting for the team, it was equally rewarding to see thousands of families and visitors enjoying the experience, as well as the strong engagement from people following Fiji Showcase online.

He says CFL's goal was to make this year's Fiji Showcase the most successful yet, and reaching the 100,000-visitor mark exceeded expectations.

Taylor adds that Fiji Showcase is not only about creating a memorable experience for visitors but also about delivering value for the hundreds of exhibitors and businesses that invest in the event by giving them the opportunity to connect with thousands of potential customers.

item.title: Drua Women crush Reds 50-17 in dominant display
item.description: The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team have secured a home semi-final after producing a dominant display to defeat the Reds 50-17, running in a series of impressive tries while showcasing their attacking flair and rock-solid defence. The Drua were in complete control during the opening half.
item.content:encoded:
The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women's team have secured a home semi-final after producing a dominant display to defeat the Reds 50-17, running in a series of impressive tries while showcasing their attacking flair and rock-solid defence.

The Drua were in complete control during the opening half.

In the 28th minute, Drua crossed over for another try to give them a commanding 21-0 lead.

Qoro sliced through the Reds' defence before delivering a perfectly timed pass to Aqela Biutu, who finished the move to extend the advantage to 26-0.

The Reds finally found a breakthrough in the 38th minute with their first try of the match, reducing the deficit to 26-5.

However, the Drua responded immediately.

Applying relentless pressure, they forced another opening and crossed over in the 39th minute to head into halftime with a comfortable 33-5 lead.

The second half began with the Reds looking for a way back into the contest.

Eva Kapani, the Reds Prop, produced a strong line break shortly after the restart, but the Drua's defence held firm to deny the home side.

Both teams created attacking opportunities during the opening stages of the half, but handling errors and strong defensive efforts prevented either side from adding to the scoreboard.

The breakthrough eventually came in the 49th minute when Litiana Vueti scored another well-worked try, stretching the Drua's lead to 40-5.

The Drua continued to apply pressure in attack, although they lost possession after building momentum in the 60th minute.

Four minutes later, the Reds mounted several attacking phases deep inside the Drua's territory, Drua side's determined defence stood tall to keep them scoreless.

The Drua then launched one of the most impressive attacks of the match in the 67th minute.

After patiently building through 11 phases, they finally broke the Reds' defence to score another try and extend their lead to 47-5.

After more than 20 phases of relentless pressure in the 73rd minute, the Reds earned a penalty and eventually crossed for a try two minutes later to cut the score to 47-10.

They added another late try in the 79th minute to narrow the margin further to 47-17.

With the final play of the match, Qoro calmly slotted a penalty goal to bring up the half-century, sealing an emphatic 50-17 victory for the Drua Women.

The convincing win highlighted the Drua's clinical finishing, disciplined defence, and ability to dominate possession, as they outclassed the Reds from start to finish.

28 th minute McDonald's women scores another try to Robanakadavu to lead 21-0

29 minute Qoro splits the defense open, delivers a pass to Aqela Biutu to score another try to extend their lead 26-0.

38 th minute Reds score their first try in 38 th minute Drua lead 26-5.

39 th minute Drua scores another try to extend their lead after pressuring the Reds 33-5.

40th minute, Reds kicks off and Eva Kapani breaks the Reds line but Drua defense too strong for her.

46th minute both team attacking but not finishing as expected.

49th minute Litiana Vueti scores another try to extend their lead 40-5.

60th minute Drua attacking Reds line showing momentum and losses possesion.

64th minute reds launch multiple attack phases on Drua line and fail to score any points after another strong defense.

67th minute Drua causing havoc attacking the Reds line into the 11 phase and scores a try to extend their lead 47-5.

73th After 20 phases, the Reds attacked the Drua line they could not score but get another chance through a penalty.

item.title: SODELPA announces 23 proposed candidates, 22 year-old AnnMary Raduva part of the list
item.description: The Social Democratic Liberal Party has today announced their first set of 23 proposed candidates for the upcoming general elections. 22 year-old youth activist AnnMary Raduva and former New Generation Party Leader, Varinava Tiko are part of the list of proposed candidates.
item.content:encoded:
The Social Democratic Liberal Party has today announced their first set of 23 proposed candidates for the upcoming general elections.

22 year-old youth activist AnnMary Raduva and former New Generation Party Leader, Varinava Tiko are part of the list of proposed candidates.

The other proposed candidates include Party Leader Aseri Radrodro, Simione Drole, Victor Fatiaki, Paterisio Nunu, Taitusi Nacolokia, Hazrat Ali, Viliame Takayawa, Pulotu Rika, Umesh Chand, Joni Dagoya, Acalia Dakua, Filipe Qio, Vishwar Jit Karan, Ro Iva Loki, Paul Peters Hazelman, Setareki Digivanua, Peniasi Rawaidranu, Imtiaz Khan, Taufa Qoro, Filimoni Bukateci and Joela Cama.

Radrodro says they will announce the other proposed candidates when the Writ of Elections is issued.

item.title: FHTA rejects claims tourism industry backed 5% Tourism Services Tax
item.description: The Fiji Hotel and Tourism Association says the tourism industry did not propose, endorse or broadly agree to the Government's pro

item.title: 48% of new HIV infections associated with high-risk drug use - Dr Mitchell
item.description: 48 percent of people who are now becoming infected with HIV are associated with high-risk drug use, with people who inject drugs and share needles and syringes at risk of HIV transmission if one person is HIV positive. This was highlighted by the Chairperson of Fiji's National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Task Force Dr Jason Mitchell during fijivillage Straight Talk with Vijay Narayan while d
item.content:encoded:
48 percent of people who are now becoming infected with HIV are associated with high-risk drug use, with people who inject drugs and share needles and syringes at risk of HIV transmission if one person is HIV positive.

This was highlighted by the Chairperson of Fiji's National HIV Outbreak and Cluster Response Task Force Dr Jason Mitchell during fijivillage Straight Talk with Vijay Narayan while discussing the HIV epidemic in Fiji.

Dr Mitchell stresses that drug use itself does not lead to HIV, but rather the risky practices associated with drug use.

Dr Mitchell also says Fiji is seeing younger people becoming infected through practices associated with intravenous drug use, with cases being reported from the age of 10 onwards.

Dr Mitchell says one of the key prevention interventions being pursued is the introduction of a needle and syringe programme.

He says there is currently no formal needle and syringe programme in Fiji, and the Government has made a serious commitment to introducing one as soon as possible.

Dr Mitchell says the task force is preparing Cabinet papers for the Minister to seek Cabinet approval for the programme.

He says people often think a needle and syringe programme is simply about distributing needles and syringes, but he says it is much more complex than that.

Dr Mitchell says it involves understanding people who inject drugs, understanding their needs, determining how needles and syringes should be distributed, identifying the types of needles and syringes that should be provided, deciding how used needles and syringes will be safely disposed off, and developing an education programme around the initiative.

He says a needle and syringe programme is one element of an effective harm reduction programme.

Dr Mitchell says effective harm reduction programmes, rehabilitation programmes for people who no longer want to use drugs, biological interventions such as pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP) , strengthening post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP), and needle and syringe programmes are all essential interventions in responding to Fiji's HIV outbreak.

He says the Government is already ensuring the resources needed to support these new programmes are procured so they are ready once the necessary approvals are received.

Dr Mitchell also says there is strong support for the proposed needle and syringe programme.

He says following a meeting with the Police Commissioner and the senior leadership team, Police are supportive of the programme, adding that Police are essential allies in an effective public health harm reduction programme.

Dr Mitchell says the task force is encouraged by the support from Government and its partners and hopes a needle and syringe programme will be underway in Fiji from January next year.

Meanwhile, in the 2026/2027 National Budget, the Government allocated $12 million towards the national HIV response, which will include efforts to combat drug use, expand awareness campaigns, procure HIV medication, strengthen testing and treatment, and invest in healthcare workforce capacity and preventive measures.

item.description: More than 2,900 residential lots and 1,800 houses are expected to be made available as Government moves to address housing challen

item.description: Thousands of people are flocking into the Vodafone 5G Saturday at the Fiji show case 2026 for some last minute shopping enjoying t

item.title: FTUC disappointed with 2026/2027 National Budget
item.description: The Fiji Trades Union Congress says it is disappointed with the 2026/2027 National Budget, claiming it offers little new relief for workers grappling with the rising cost of living. National Secretary Felix Anthony says the Budget places greater emphasis on supporting businesses than workers, with no increase in the national minimum wage despite ongoing financial pressures faced by many households
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Trades Union Congress says it is disappointed with the 2026/2027 National Budget, claiming it offers little new relief for workers grappling with the rising cost of living.

National Secretary Felix Anthony says the Budget places greater emphasis on supporting businesses than workers, with no increase in the national minimum wage despite ongoing financial pressures faced by many households.

Finance Minister Esrom Immanuel has announced that $100,000 has been allocated for reviewing and conducting further consultations on the minimum wage and fair wages during the next fiscal year.

Anthony says wh ile $100,000 has been allocated for further consultations on the minimum wage next year, workers need immediate relief rather than more discussions.

He says the Government moved quickly to assist businesses affected by higher fuel costs but failed to provide comparable support for workers.

The Finance Minister also announced a temporary 2 percentage point reduction in the Fiji National Provident Fund (FNPF) employer contribution rate, effective from August 1st, 2026, where employer contributions will decrease from 10 percent to 8 percent, while employee contributions remain locked at 8 percent.

Anthony criticised this decision arguing that the move effectively amounts to a two percent pay cut for workers.

He claims the reduction will save the Government about $30 million while workers collectively stand to lose hundreds of millions of dollars in retirement savings at a time when many families are struggling to cope with the cost of living.

Anthony further expressed disappointment that the Government did not include funding to compensate former Water Authority of Fiji workers who were terminated in 2019.

He says the compensation was announced in the 2024/2025 National Budget but is yet to be delivered, describing the omission as a breach of the Government's commitment.

While welcoming investments in infrastructure, Anthony says the overall Budget favours state-owned enterprises and the private sector over workers and their families.

He says the assistance highlighted in the Budget largely consists of measures announced in previous years rather than new initiatives to address current economic challenges.

Anthony adds that workers cannot rely on past assistance to meet today's needs and says maintaining existing tax rates and previously announced support does not provide meaningful relief in the current economic climate.

item.description: Pet owners are being encouraged to bring their dogs and cats to a free desexing clinic in Suva today, as part of efforts to improv

item.title: Roko Tui Bau says Fiji needs leaders who are prepared to confront reality honestly, and prioritize people over politics
item.description: Across the country, public attention has increasingly been consumed by political disputes, court rulings, constitutional debates,
